title: Capitalizing Interest on Self-Constructed Assets
event: Hollis Co. self-constructs a building with weighted-average accumulated expenditures of $400,000. It has a $300,000 specific construction loan at 10% and other general debt at 12%. Actual total interest incurred was $60,000. Compute the interest to capitalize.

computationPaths.narration: Avoidable interest: the specific loan covers expenditures first at its rate; the overflow uses the general-debt rate. Capitalize the LESSER of avoidable and actual.

memorize.body: Avoidable interest = specific loan × its rate + (WAAE − specific loan) × weighted-average rate on other debt. Capitalize min(avoidable, actual).
Weighted-average ACCUMULATED expenditures — money spent early accrues more interest than December's spending. The weighting is often the sub-computation.

questions.prompt: WAAE $400,000; specific loan $300,000 at 10%; other debt 12%; actual interest $60,000. Interest capitalized = ?

distractors.feedback: That's ACTUAL interest. Capitalization is capped at the AVOIDABLE amount tied to construction: 42,000.
You applied 10% to all 400,000. The specific loan only covers 300,000 — the 100,000 overflow uses the 12% general rate.
